title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, leading the Braves over the Red Sox 3-2.
The Red Sox fell to a season record of 18-26 after their loss to the Braves.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer hit a solo homer to tie the game in the seventh inning.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A reunion between the Patriots and Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to a pending trade for A.J. Brown.
Stefon Diggs expressed interest in returning to New England after posting a 1,000-yard season with the Patriots.
The Patriots are expected to take on a $32 million salary for A.J. Brown, limiting cap space for Diggs.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police are asking for help finding 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, last seen Tuesday near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester.
LaGrant-Gomes, described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, was reported missing after failing to return home.
Police are actively investigating and ask anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit tips anonymously.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on May 10.
The victims, both 42, were shot outside a Kingsdale Street home; the woman suffered life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder; he is held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA and 1.08 WHIP in 39 starts in 2026.
Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts, former Red Sox, continue successful careers, contributing to their teams' performance.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs with 18, highlighting missed opportunities for the Red Sox to re-sign him.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony, 22, has been out with a right wrist sprain since May 7, missing nine games.
Anthony no longer wears a wrist brace and played catch before Friday's game against the Atlanta Braves.
He has yet to resume swinging a bat but aims to start soon based on his recovery progress.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A white Jeep accidentally drove into the BrightPath daycare in Norwood, Massachusetts on Friday morning.
No injuries were reported, and children were safely evacuated following the crash into the building's glass entrance.
The daycare was temporarily closed for structural assessment after the driver accidentally accelerated instead of stopping.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will have a 10-7 record for the 2026 NFL season.
The team's 2026 schedule includes challenging matches against Seattle, Denver, and Buffalo.
The Patriots' projected record contrasts with their previous 14-3 season, influenced by 2025's easier schedule.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short,' featuring Martin Short and Catherine O'Hara, is now streaming on Netflix.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2 is available on Netflix, hosted by Colin Jost with new team formats.
'Tucci in Italy' Season 2, focused on Italian cuisine, streams on Disney+ and Hulu.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Wu's $4.9 billion budget proposal faces potential rejection from Boston City Council due to proposed spending cuts.
Councilors can amend but not increase the total budget, with a deadline to act by June 10, before the budget defaults.
Boston faces a $50 million deficit while Mayor Wu warns against increasing budget due to fiscal responsibility concerns.
